topic: Someone discovers their friend has been lying about their job
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a poorly aimed projectile. It was a formal notification from OmniCorp, detailing my recent 'performance review.' The title read 'Contract Termination.' My stomach plummeted, a cold, sickening drop. I immediately called Liam, my supposed confidant, needing an explanation, needing reassurance that this was some elaborate prank. When he finally answered, his voice was breezy, almost too light. "What's up? Rough day?" I pushed the screen toward him, my hands shaking slightly. He glanced at the screen, a flicker of something unreadable crossing his face before he quickly averted his gaze. "Oh, that? Just some internal restructuring. Nothing to worry about, man. I've got things under control." The casual dismissal of the catastrophic news sent a wave of hot, prickly anger through me. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small living room, my jaw tight. "Restructuring? Liam, this is my job! You told me you were in that senior analyst role!"

[story 2]

I found the discrepancy tucked away in a shared cloud document—a meticulously organized spreadsheet detailing project milestones. The dates didn't align. Liam had claimed to be working on the 'Phoenix Initiative' during that entire period, yet the official project logs showed he was on vacation, completely untracked. A slow burn began in my chest, starting as a tight knot that tightened with every contradictory piece of evidence. I confronted him at the coffee shop, the clatter of ceramic cups suddenly sounding deafening. "Look at this," I said, shoving the tablet across the table. My voice was low, dangerously steady. Liam fiddled with his sleeve, avoiding eye contact. "It's just a scheduling mix-up, Sarah. Things get messy when you're juggling multiple things." The way he shifted in his seat, the way his eyes darted toward the exit, confirmed everything. I felt a sudden, sharp pressure behind my eyes, a need to shout, to dismantle every flimsy excuse he’d ever offered.

[story 3]

It started with a misplaced piece of company jargon. Liam used the term 'Synergy Optimization' constantly in our conversations, painting a picture of high-level corporate maneuvering. But when I cross-referenced it with the public company filings, the term simply didn't exist in any capacity related to his department. My breath hitched. I spent the next hour digging through old Slack messages, tracing his supposed daily activities. Every alibi felt hollow, brittle under scrutiny. When I finally cornered him in the dimly lit hallway by the water cooler, I didn't raise my voice. Instead, I leaned in close, my shadow falling over him. "Stop. Just stop talking. I know what you're hiding." His composure fractured instantly; his face flushed a deep crimson, and he stammered something about needing space. Watching him shrink under the weight of my certainty made my fists clench at my sides, the tension vibrating through my arms. He looked cornered, and the sheer audacity of his deception made my throat feel constricted.
